body.text: Crypto News Bot — Daily Digest

Six federal agencies (OCC, FDIC, NCUA, Treasury, FinCEN, OFAC) missed the July 18 statutory deadline to finalize GENIUS Act stablecoin rules, leaving reserve and AML standards unsettled a year after passage.
Stablecoin News
GENIUS Act rulemaking deadline missed
As of July 19, none of the six federal regulators had finalized implementing rules for the GENIUS Act, one year after it was signed into law.
Circle wins OCC trust bank charter
The OCC approved Circle to operate as a national trust bank, letting it manage USDC reserves (over $73B in circulation) directly; shares jumped on the news.
Visa launches stablecoin platform for banks

Visa unveiled the Visa Stablecoin Platform, letting financial firms issue and manage stablecoins; it will initially back Open USD, a new coin from a 140+ member consortium including Stripe and Mastercard.
Stablecoin volume tops $7.5T, passes ACH
On-chain stablecoin settlement volume climbed past the U.S. ACH network, with market cap now around $316.7B, underscoring stablecoins' shift into mainstream payment rails.
Hacking & Security Incidents
Ostium hit by $18M oracle exploit
An attacker abused a registered price-feed forwarder on the Arbitrum-based RWA perps exchange Ostium to fake trade profits, draining about $18M in USDC on July 15.
Across Protocol suffers first Solana-side attack
Across paused and then restored Solana deposits after its first-ever attack on that chain; no user funds were lost, with relayer losses absorbed by Risk Labs.
Cascade and DeFiTuna add to $20M weekly loss tally
A locked-fund drain at Cascade and a Solana lending shortfall at DeFiTuna pushed the week's confirmed DeFi damage above $20M across four separate incidents.
Ethical hackers flag $70B Aptos risk

Security firm Hexens disclosed a now-patched Aptos consensus flaw that could have been exploited for under $1,000 with a near-90% success rate, putting billions in assets and bridges at theoretical risk.
